taskDescription: Arrange the branches of the internal iliac artery from its origin to its terminal divisions in correct anatomical order
paragraphs: Internal iliac artery arises from the common iliac artery at the level of the sacroiliac joint
Divides into anterior and posterior divisions at the upper border of the greater sciatic foramen
Posterior division gives off iliolumbar, lateral sacral, and superior gluteal arteries
Anterior division gives off the obturator, inferior vesical (male) or uterine (female), and middle rectal arteries
Anterior division continues as the internal pudendal artery supplying the perineum
Anterior division terminates as the inferior gluteal artery exiting through the greater sciatic foramen below piriformis
